exemplar.title:
In comparison to 2022, how did the takeover of Newcastle United Football Club affect the

detailedExemplarData.title:
In comparison to 2022, how did the takeover of Newcastle United Football Club affect the

subject.title: Business Management
subject.description:
**IB Business Management** is a **Group 3** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **business fundamentals, human resource management, finance and accounts, marketing, and operations management**, with a strong focus on **case study analysis, real-world business applications, and strategic decision-making**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2024**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| **Assessment** | **Format** | **SL** | **HL** | **Weighting** |
|----------------|------------|--------|--------|---------------|
| **Paper 1** | Case study-based exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 25% |
| **Paper 2** | Core themes exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 3** | Social enterprise case study | N/A | 1h 15min | HL: 25% |
| **Internal Assessment** | Written commentary (SL) / Research project (HL) | Various | Various | SL: 30%, HL: 20% |

strengths: Clear definition and consistent reference to the key concept of change throughout the introduction and conclusion
Strong contextual integration of the concept via comparison with other club takeovers
Explicit linking of revenue, cost and strategy discussions back to organisational transformation
weaknesses: Integration of the concept is only briefly applied when interpreting ratio analysis
The framing statement in the introduction could more clearly signal how âchangeâ will guide each subsequent analytical section
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Strand evidence shows a strong understanding and good analytical integration of the key concept, meeting band 4 descriptors. While the concept of change is central and frequently revisited, it is not yet seamlessly woven into every analytical tool, preventing a band 5 award. A 4/5 accurately reflects this high but not pervasive integration.

strengths: Uses three distinct and relevant supporting documents in line with quantitative requirements
Financial statements and regulatory sources supply concrete figures underpinning analyses
weaknesses: Range of source types is narrow, lacking academic or industry-wide reports
Depth of commentary on non-financial aspects is limited, reducing contextual richness
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The submission meets the basic requirement of 3â5 documents with solid relevance (band 3), but the narrow range and moderate depth align more closely with band 2 expectations. A 2/4 reflects adequate but not fully in-depth support.

strengths: Appropriate selection of financial tools (profit-and-loss comparisons, profitability ratios) aligned to the research question
Clear presentation of formulas and calculated results enhances methodological transparency
weaknesses: Absence of strategic or qualitative models (e.g., SWOT, Ansoff) limits analytical breadth
Interpretation of results lacks industry benchmarks, reducing comparative insight
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Tools are adequately chosen and applied (band 3), but the limited toolkit and uneven depth of application align closer to band 2. A score of 2/4 reflects this adequate yet restricted use of theories and tools.
feedback.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

strengths: Thorough use of revenue and cost data with accurate figures in narrative analysis
Insightful strategic comparison between frugal and investment-driven ownership
Consideration of regulatory (FFP/PSR) constraints in context
weaknesses: Analysis sometimes remains descriptive rather than evaluative, especially on long-term sustainability
Lacks benchmarking against competitor clubs or league averages to contextualize performance
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Data selection is sufficient and analysis is mostly effective (band 4), yet occasional descriptive passages and missing comparative benchmarks suggest more moderate evaluative depth. A band 3 award (3/5) accurately reflects this mixed analysis quality.

strengths: Conclusions are fully consistent with the bodyâs evidence and accurately restate key findings
Explicitly and directly answers the research question in a clear, labeled section
weaknesses: Does not acknowledge limitations or potential future risks affecting financial sustainability
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The conclusion meets all top-band descriptorsâconsistency with evidence and explicit answer to the research questionâwarranting a full 3/3.

strengths: Logical, coherent structure throughout: introduction, context, analysis, conclusion
Clear section headings and a detailed table of contents facilitate navigation
weaknesses: Table of contents lacks page numbers, which would enhance usability
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The assessment demonstrates an appropriate and consistent organizational structure, fulfilling the band 2 criteria for a full 2/2.

strengths: All required presentation elements are present (title page, word count, research question, key concept, TOC, bibliography)
Formatting is generally clean, and supporting documents are clearly listed
weaknesses: Citation formatting is inconsistent and some bibliography entries break across lines
Table layouts include minor duplication errors and lack consistent hyperlink formatting
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: With every mandatory element included and no critical omissions, the work earns the top band for presentation. A 2/2 reflects this complete but stylistically improvable presentation.

strandSelections.reasoning: The IA follows a logical structure: introduction, context, analysis sections keyed to the table of contents, followed by conclusion and bibliography. Headings are clear, and progression is easy to follow, matching the descriptor for âappropriate structure throughoutâ and earning the top score available for this strand.

The chosen documents are directly relevant (club financial results, Swiss Ramble analysis, prior-year statements) and provide detailed figures that underpin the studentâs calculations. Nonetheless, the range of ideas is somewhat narrowâlargely financial and from a limited set of authorsâso depth is solid but not truly âin-depth with a wide rangeâ. Band 3 therefore best fits the evidence.

All required elementsâtitle page with word count, research question, key concept, table of contents, section headings, supporting documents list and full bibliographyâare present. No mandatory component is missing, so the highest band for presentation is warranted.

The bibliography identifies three documents designated as supporting documents, satisfying the 3â5 requirement. No excess documents are listed, and each is distinct. This places the work in the middle-upper bands; band 3 is appropriate for meeting, but not exceeding, the quantitative expectation.

The profit-and-loss table is used to compute growth figures and ratios, and FFP rules are cited to explain spending constraints. While application is clearly linked to the research question, depth of interpretation is uneven; for example, ratio results are stated but not benchmarked against industry norms. Thus relevance is clear but application remains âadequateâ, fitting band 3.

The student selects full revenue-and-cost data for two years, detailed expenditure breakdowns and supporting ratio computations. Figures are quoted accurately and used within the narrative, indicating âsufficientâ rather than merely âadequateâ use of data. Occasional gaps (e.g., no competitor benchmarks) prevent classification as âeffectiveâ in the highest sense, so band 4 is most appropriate.

Analysis links revenue growth to strategic initiatives, explains expenditure changes via player investment and interprets ratio trends. The student comments on regulatory influence and evaluates effectiveness of spending. While largely sound, the analysis sometimes stops at description (e.g., limited discussion of long-term sustainability), and integration of the key concept is intermittent. This aligns with âmostly effective analysis with some integrationââband 4.

The final paragraph explicitly answers the research question, stating how revenue and expenditure changed in 2023 relative to 2022 and attributing this to the takeover. The response is direct and unambiguous, satisfying the descriptor for full addressing of the question.

Tools include profit-and-loss comparison, profitability ratios and reference to FFP/PSR regulations. This constitutes an adequate but relatively limited toolkit; strategic or qualitative models (SWOT, Ansoff, etc.) are absent. The selection is therefore âadequateâ rather than âeffectiveâ, matching band 3.

The conclusion restates numerical improvements and ties them back to the PIF strategy and concept of change, mirroring evidence presented in the body. No contradictions are evident. This fulfilment of evidence-consistency expectations justifies the top band within this criterion.

All discussion centres on Newcastle United; financial statements, ratio calculations, regulations, strategy comparisons and quotations from executives are organisation-specific. Evidence is continually drawn from the clubâs own accounts, official statements and recognised commentators, showing a comprehensive, embedded link to the organisation at every stage. This level of contextual anchoring fulfils the descriptor of a âfully integratedâ connection, aligning with the top band.

The student explicitly selects the key concept of change, defines it in the context of footballâclub takeovers and refers back to it in the introduction, analytical sections and conclusion. The narrative repeatedly highlights the shift from Mike Ashley to PIF ownership and links each revenue-, cost- and strategy-discussion to this organisational transformation. However, the concept is not evenly woven into every analytical tool (e.g., it is mentioned only briefly within the ratio analysis and not used as a lens for interpreting all data). This demonstrates a strong, but not fully pervasive, integrationâconsistent with band 4 rather than the âthroughoutâ requirement of band 5.

annotations.comment: The student clearly identifies âchangeâ as the key concept but could briefly outline in this section how it will consistently frame the subsequent analysis to strengthen integration throughout.
The research question is concise, focused on specific metrics (revenue and expenditure) and timeframes, providing a clear foundation for the investigation.
The table of contents lays out a clear and logical progression of sections, facilitating easy navigation through the assessmentâs structure.
Including page numbers for each ToC entry would improve usability and ensure readers can quickly locate specific sections.
The discussion of smaller club takeovers effectively illustrates the key concept of change by comparing Newcastleâs scenario with similar cases like Manchester City and PSG.
The student clearly outlines the intended analytical tools (financial accounts and profitability ratios), demonstrating thoughtful selection aligned with the research question.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: While the PIF takeover is well described, explicitly tying this context back to the concept of change here would reinforce thematic coherence from the outset.
The narrative asserts a direct correlation between squad value and performance; consider citing empirical studies or league data to substantiate this claim.
The use of supporting document 3 to establish baseline revenue figures demonstrates strong sourcing and relevance of financial data.
Consider benchmarking Newcastleâs revenue growth percentages against league averages or competitor clubs to contextualize performance more robustly.
The table contains duplicate âCommercialâ rows which could confuse readers; revise the layout for clarity and accuracy.
Although EBITDA and operating profit are presented, deeper commentary on the divergence between these metrics would strengthen the evaluative analysis.
When discussing the PIFâs calculated investments, explicitly referencing the key concept of change in this context would reinforce thematic integration.
The description of FFP/PSR constraints is accurate; however, adding specific references to the governing bodyâs official regulation document would enhance depth and credibility.
The strategic comparison between Ashleyâs frugal approach and PIFâs investment-driven strategy is presented clearly, enriching evaluative insight.
In this discussion of strategies, reiterating how each reflects the concept of change would deepen integration of the key concept across analytical sections.
Section headings are specific and descriptive, guiding the reader effectively through the revenue analysis framework.
Some reference entries span multiple lines with inconsistent formatting; consolidate each citation into a single, coherent entry.
The inclusion of up-to-date regulatory sources (e.g., Premier League FFP rules) demonstrates relevance and solid supporting documentation.
The profitability ratios are well calculated, with clear formula descriptions and precise values, showcasing effective application of financial tools.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: Link the observed expenditure growth back to the constraints of FFP/PSR regulations to demonstrate how regulatory tools influenced PIFâs spending strategy.
The introduction of profitability ratios is clear and positions the reader for detailed metric-based evaluation of the PIFâs effectiveness.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: Providing both formulas and calculated results in a single table enhances transparency and demonstrates rigorous methodological application.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: The conclusion section is clearly labeled and signals a coherent synthesis of the assessmentâs findings.
The conclusion effectively summarizes results but could be strengthened by acknowledging limitations or potential industry risks for Newcastleâs future financial sustainability.
While the supporting documents are relevant, expanding the range to include academic journals or regulatory reports would deepen the evidence base.
Citation formatting is inconsistent; adopt a uniform style (e.g., APA or MLA) and include access dates for all online sources to ensure professionalism.
URLs should be hyperlinked or formatted on a single line to avoid break errors and maintain legibility in the bibliography.
Include publication year details and source attribution for âHow billionaire owners changed European footballâ to improve citation completeness.
